label: Link
purpose: Unified anchor component. Covers all link contexts across the site through a single variant prop. Always renders as <a> — never a button. Replaces 6 previously ad-hoc link styles (nav-link, hero-social-link, footer-link, footer-contact-link, social-pill, article-back-link). The external prop adds target and rel attributes automatically.

props.name: href
props.description: Destination URL. Always required — Link has no buttonless fallback. Use Button for actions that do not navigate.

props.name: variant
props.description: Controls which CSS class is applied and which surface the link is designed for. Surface matching is mandatory — see accessibility rules.

props.name: external
props.description: When true, adds target='_blank' rel='noopener noreferrer'. Required for all links that open in a new tab. Do not add target='_blank' manually and omit external — the rel will be missing.

props.name: class
props.description: Appended to the generated class string. Use for one-off overrides only.

props.name: ...rest
props.description: Spread onto the <a> element. Use for aria-label, data-*, aria-current.

variants.description: No CSS class applied — relies entirely on the global a {} rule in tokens.css. For inline links in body copy, articles, or any light surface. The global rule applies automatically to bare <a> elements too.
Inline links within dark-surface sections (hero social row, dark callout blocks). Mirror of default — same underline pattern, inverted colors.
Navigation items styled as pill-buttons. Used in the header nav. Always renders as <a> — if an action needs a click handler instead of navigation, use a raw <button> with class='nav-link'.
Back/return navigation from detail pages. Carries margin-bottom: 40px — intentional for the article/page header context. Use only at the top of a dark page header where back navigation belongs.
Plain text navigation items in the footer bottom bar. No pill shape — just text color transitions. Distinct from back: no margin, no arrow implied.

renderRule: Always renders as <a>. There are no conditional element types. If you need a <button> that looks like nav-link or footer-nav-link (for onClick scroll behavior), use a raw <button> with the CSS class directly — Link cannot be used for this.
accessibility: href is always required — do not pass '#' or 'javascript:void(0)' as href
Surface matching is mandatory: default (--fg-primary) on dark surfaces produces ~1.1:1 contrast — invisible. on-dark/nav/back/footer-nav use white/muted white — invisible on light surfaces
external prop must be used for any link opening in a new tab — it adds rel='noopener noreferrer' which prevents window.opener exploitation
back variant carries margin-bottom: 40px — if used outside an article/page header context, override with class or a wrapper
default variant applies no class — the element will render with no class attribute. This is correct; do not add class='default' as a workaround
For links with icon-only content (no visible text), always add aria-label via ...rest
whenToUse: default: inline links in body copy, diary article content, or any light surface where a prominent underlined link is appropriate
on-dark: inline links within dark sections (hero social row, dark callout blocks)
nav: navigation items that look like pills and navigate to anchors or pages (header nav links)
back: 'Back' or 'return' navigation at the top of a detail page (article back link, DS page top bar)
footer-nav: plain text navigation links in the footer bottom bar or DS sub-nav
whenNotToUse: default on dark surfaces — --fg-primary (black) text on --bg-dark is invisible
on-dark, nav, back, or footer-nav on light surfaces — all use white or muted white text, unreadable on light backgrounds
nav when you need a visible URL in browser status bar — nav is styled as a button shape; use default for traditional visible link appearance
back for any link that is not backwards navigation — the bottom margin assumes article/page header context
Inside React files (.jsx) — Link.astro cannot be imported into JSX islands
In place of Button when an action does not navigate — Link always renders <a>; actions without destinations need <button>

knownRawCSSMimics.migrationNote: Hero.astro is an Astro file — can import Link and use <Link variant='on-dark' external>.

knownRawCSSMimics.runtimeCount: N (one per nav item, onClick scroll-to-section)
knownRawCSSMimics.migrationNote: Two blockers: (1) Header.jsx is a React island — cannot import Link.astro. (2) These are <button> elements driving onClick scroll behavior; Link always renders <a> and cannot carry click-handler-only scroll navigation.
Same dual blocker as Header nav-link: React island + onClick scroll behavior. Must remain <button> with raw CSS class.
Footer.jsx is a React island — cannot import Link.astro. Could be migrated if Footer were converted to Astro.

knownRawCSSMimics.runtimeCount: 3 (top back home, bottom back home, bottom scroll-to-top)
knownRawCSSMimics.migrationNote: diary/[slug].astro is an Astro file. Note: the scroll-to-top link (line 50) uses onclick='window.scrollTo...' — if migrated to Link, the onclick behavior would need to be handled differently (data attribute + inline script, or keep as raw <a>).

usageConstraints.reason: Link.astro cannot be imported into .jsx React islands. Additionally, nav-link and footer-nav-link as <button> elements are permanently blocked from migration because Link always renders <a> — the <button> element type is required for scroll-to-section behavior.
